mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal yields a negative information coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or flat during post-shock recovery periods when INDPRO rises, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 100 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 235 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and forces equity valuation compression, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive in over 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235, or the threshold signal shows zero predictive power in a logistic regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that raises wage inflation expectations, prompting short-term equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion phase signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive when GDPC1 growth decelerates in late-cycle regimes, or the hypothesis fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output that improves supply chain efficiency and corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of months with rising INDPRO, or the signal produces a negative rank correlation with market returns over a 5-year sample.
